medications.indication_rationale: There is no specific drug cure; management is supportive pacing and treating any identified cause. Analgesia for aches is optional.

medications.drug_name: high-dose caffeine / stimulants to push through
medications.indication_rationale: Caffeine/stimulants to 'push through' worsen the crash cycle and cause palpitations — not appropriate.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use strict activity 'pacing' — stop activities before symptoms rise, break tasks into small chunks with rest, and avoid the push-crash cycle; avoid strenuous exercise for now.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Pacing prevents post-exertional crashes and supports gradual recovery.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Prioritise sleep hygiene; rest in a quiet space during the day without necessarily sleeping; consider temporary help with meals/energy-heavy tasks.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Supports recovery and conserves limited energy.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like post-viral fatigue — your body is still recovering from the infection. It's a diagnosis we confirm by first checking some blood tests (and, given your symptoms after a virus, it's sensible to have a heart tracing/ECG) to rule out other causes.

The key is pacing: do a little, rest before you're exhausted, and don't 'push through' — that causes the multi-day crashes. Caffeine won't help and can cause palpitations. Recovery is usually gradual over weeks to months.

Call 000 / go to ED if you get chest pain, breathlessness at rest, a racing or irregular heartbeat, or you faint. See your GP promptly for unexplained weight loss, drenching night sweats, persistent fever, or new lumps or weakness.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Blood/ECG results; establish a pacing and graded return plan; reconsider red flags.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Cardiac features — 000; weight loss/night sweats/fever/focal signs — urgent GP.

must_include_items: Treat as a diagnosis of exclusion — arrange baseline bloods ± ECG and screen red flags
Cardiac (myocarditis) safety-net — cannot be excluded remotely
Activity pacing (avoid push-crash); avoid caffeine/'pushing through'
Organic red-flag safety-net (weight loss, night sweats, fever, focal signs)
acceptable_alternatives: GP review to arrange/interpret bloods and ECG
errors_of_omission: Labelling post-viral fatigue without excluding organic causes / no bloods
errors_of_commission: Advising 'push through'/graded exercise as a cure
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires treating it as a diagnosis of exclusion (bloods ± ECG, red-flag screen), a cardiac safety-net, and pacing advice.
